forecast-period.text: A high pressure system over the Tasman sea extends a weak ridge over SA. A trough over central WA extends over southwest SA, and is forecast to move across the west and south later Tuesday, pushing into the northeast of SA on Wednesday. Another high pressure system is expected to become established south of the Bight late in the week.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

area.description: Far West Coast: WA-SA Border to Fowlers Bay

forecast-period.text: North to northwesterly 15 to 20 knots shifting southwesterly in the early afternoon.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres offshore.
Southwesterly 1.5 metres.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm.
South to southwesterly 10 to 15 knots becoming variable about 10 knots in the morning.
Around 1 metre, decreasing below 0.5 metres during the morning, then increasing to below 1 metre around midday.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Mostly sunny.
Variable about 10 knots becoming southeasterly 10 to 15 knots during the afternoon.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Mostly sunny.

area.description: Upper West Coast: Fowlers Bay to Elliston

forecast-period.text: North to northwesterly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 25 knots south of Streaky Bay in the late morning. Winds turning west to southwesterly in the late afternoon.
1 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 1 to 1.5 metres.
Becoming cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the afternoon and evening.
Southwesterly 10 to 15 knots decreasing to about 10 knots in the early morning.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Sunny.
Variable about 10 knots becoming southerly 10 to 15 knots during the afternoon then tending southeasterly during the evening.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 2 metres during the evening.
Mostly sunny.

area.description: Lower West Coast: Elliston to Cape Carnot

forecast-period.text: Northerly 15 to 20 knots turning westerly in the late afternoon.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 1.5 metres.
Becoming cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the afternoon and evening.
West to southwesterly 10 to 15 knots decreasing to about 10 knots in the morning.
Around 1 metre, decreasing below 0.5 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 4 metres during the morning.
Mostly sunny.
Variable about 10 knots.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy.
Northerly 15 to 20 knots turning west to northwesterly in the late afternoon.
1 to 1.5 metres.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1.5 metres later in the evening.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers in the afternoon and evening. The chance of a thunderstorm in the west in the afternoon and evening.
Westerly 15 to 20 knots decreasing to about 10 knots in the late afternoon.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre during the morning.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning, then tending westerly 3 to 4 metres during the afternoon.
Mostly sunny.
West to southwesterly 10 to 15 knots turning southerly during the evening.
Below 1 metre.
Westerly 3 metres, tending southwesterly 2.5 metres during the morning.
Mostly sunny.
Northerly 15 to 20 knots turning west to northwesterly in the evening.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres west of Kangaroo Island.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
Westerly 15 to 20 knots decreasing to about 10 knots in the late afternoon.
1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 1 metre around midday.
Southwesterly 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning, then tending westerly 3 to 4 metres by early evening.
Mostly sunny.
Westerly 10 to 15 knots turning south to southwesterly during the afternoon.
Below 1 metre.
Westerly 2.5 to 3 metres, tending southwesterly 2.5 metres during the afternoon or evening.
Partly cloudy.

area.description: Upper South East Coast: Murray Mouth to Cape Jaffa

forecast-period.text: North to northeasterly 10 to 15 knots tending north to northwesterly during the afternoon and evening. Winds reaching up to 20 knots offshore in the late morning and early afternoon and again in the late evening.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres offshore.
Southwesterly 1.5 metres inshore, increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres offshore.
Mostly sunny.
Northwesterly 10 to 15 knots turning west to southwesterly below 10 knots early in the morning.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the afternoon.
60% chance of showers in the early morning. Sunny day.
West to northwesterly about 10 knots becoming south to southwesterly during the afternoon.
Below 1 metre.
Westerly 2 to 3 metres, tending southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres during the evening.
Mostly sunny.

area.description: Lower South East Coast: Cape Jaffa to SA-VIC Border

forecast-period.text: Northerly 15 to 20 knots.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres offshore.
Southwesterly 2 metres.
Partly cloudy.
Northwesterly 10 to 15 knots, reaching up to 20 knots south of Port MacDonnell early in the morning. Winds turning westerly early in the morning.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres, tending westerly 2.5 to 3 metres later in the evening.
60% chance of showers in the early morning. Sunny day.
West to northwesterly 15 to 20 knots turning southwesterly 10 to 15 knots during the evening.
Below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Port MacDonnell during the afternoon.
Westerly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Partly cloudy. 50% chance of showers.
Northerly 15 to 20 knots turning westerly in the late evening.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
South to southwesterly around 1 metre.
Mostly sunny day. 60% chance of showers in the evening.
Westerly 10 to 15 knots turning southerly 15 to 20 knots during the morning and early afternoon.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres north of Cowell to Wallaroo during the afternoon.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Cowell to Wallaroo.
The chance of a thunderstorm north of Cowell to Wallaroo in the morning. Mostly sunny afternoon.
Southerly 10 to 15 knots becoming variable about 10 knots during the morning then becoming southerly 15 to 25 knots during the day.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the evening.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres south of Port Neill to Port Victoria.
Partly cloudy.
Northerly 10 to 15 knots, reaching up to 20 knots south of Port Vincent to Brighton in the late morning.
Around 1 metre.
Southeast to southwesterly below 0.5 metres.
Mostly sunny.
Westerly 10 to 15 knots turning southerly in the late morning and afternoon.
Below 1 metre.
West to southwesterly below 1 metre.
60% chance of showers in the early morning. Mostly sunny day.
Variable below 10 knots becoming southerly 10 to 15 knots during the day.
Below 1 metre.
South to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Mostly sunny.
Northerly 15 to 20 knots turning westerly 10 to 15 knots in the late evening.
1 to 1.5 metres.
West to southwesterly below 1 metre.
Becoming cloudy. 60% chance of showers in the evening.
Westerly 15 to 20 knots turning south to southwesterly 10 to 15 knots in the afternoon then becoming variable below 10 knots in the late evening.
Around 1 metre.
West to southwesterly around 1 metre, increasing to 1.5 to 2.5 metres during the afternoon.
Mostly sunny.
Variable below 10 knots becoming west to southwesterly 10 to 15 knots during the day then tending south to southwesterly during the afternoon.
Below 1 metre.
West to southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy.
